notes: This pass was led with the new flow: tiny-veneer scan first, then bank-aware target checks on the strongest raw candidates inside 18FD..1A5F.

The eye-catching JSR/RTS pattern at 1989..198C is not promoted: it targets C3:11BA, which lies inside the already frozen post-marker data block, so it is treated as a false wrapper signature inside mixed content rather than a real veneer.

C3:1A1A has a raw same-bank caller from C3:0C7D and C3:1A60 has one from C3:0CAA, making them better candidate seams than the earlier raw targets; however the 1A1A pocket still does not stabilize into a trustworthy owner/helper start under cautious byte inspection.
The next live seam is moved to 1A60 because it is the next better-supported candidate pocket after freezing the unstable lead-in cluster from 18FD..1A5F.
